reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures all components of the informal statement: (1) `[IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing A]` encodes "A is a local CM ring"; (2) `[IsRegularLocalRing B]` together with `{f : B →+* A}` and `hf : Function.Surjective f` encode "A is a quotient of a regular local ring" — this universal-over-B,f form is logically equivalent to an existential since the conclusion doesn't depend on B or f; (3) `[IsDomain A]` + `[UniqueFactorizationMonoid A]` is the standard Mathlib encoding of "UFD" (since `UniqueFactorizationMonoid` alone doesn't imply `Nontrivial`); (4) `IsGorensteinRing A` is defined via localization at every maximal ideal, but since A is local with a unique maximal ideal whose localization is isomorphic to A, this is logically equivalent to `IsGorensteinLocalRing A`, matching the informal "A is Gorenstein". No hypotheses are silently added or dropped.
